mappings.rationaleAI: Work as a social, economic, and psychological phenomenon covering capitalism, Weber's Protestant ethic, and the consequences of unemployment relates broadly to the organization of work (A) and the psychological and social aspects of rationalized work (C.1).

Management theory covering organizational behavior, motivation, job satisfaction, and leadership from Mayo through Porter addresses the application of scientific methods to managerial functions (B) and operations research applied to organized systems (B.1).

Interdisciplinary analysis of organizations from prehistory to contemporary forms covering contingency theory, management, competition, and supply chain dynamics addresses the organization of work (A) and scientific methods in management (B).
Engineering harnessing natural phenomena for infrastructure including water systems, buildings, and railways represents systems engineering utilizing scientific and technological knowledge (B.2).

British industrialization covering steam power, textile machinery, and iron production demonstrates changes in production techniques from 16th-18th century (A.4), mass production by product (A.5), and the use of machines as labour replacements (A.6).
Manufacturing and warehouse automation replacing human labor directly addresses machines as replacements for labour (A.6) and the effects of automation on mankind (C.1).
AI applications in everyday life and technological challenges of intelligent systems connect to machines as labour replacements (A.6) and the psychological and social aspects of automation (C.1).

Innovation through business strategy and industrial production covering creative destruction, economies of scale, and disruptive technologies relates to mass production (A.5) and machines as labor replacements (A.6).
Capitalism traced from 17th-century commerce through industrialization covers changes in production techniques from 16th-18th centuries (A.4) and mass production through industrial capitalism (A.5).
Game theory as a mathematical framework for strategic decision-making directly connects to game theory as a systems-design tool (B.3.b).
HR management governing employer-employee relations, labor practices, and workforce strategy addresses the organization of work (A) and scientific methods applied to managerial functions (B).
Leadership theory covering decision-making, the relationship between leaders and followers, and management practice addresses the application of scientific methods to managerial functions (B).
Evolution of computing from calculation aids through automatic electronic computation demonstrates the trajectory of machines as labor replacements (A.6).

Early human societies covering the invention of agriculture and development of cities relate to the organization of work in the prehistoric world (A.1) including initial communal organization and specialization.

Greek city-state civilization with its political institutions and social hierarchies relates to the organization of work in the ancient world (A.2) including the effect of social classes on labour organization.
